source: Fan, Cross-Border Stablecoin Architecture v3 (Twelve Jurisdictions, June 2026), §7 (Implementation and Open Questions)
note: Sections 7.1-7.4 are the four developments the paper identifies as determining which architectural options become operationally viable; 7.5 is the wholesale-settlement infrastructure-implications question. Each is preserved with its conditional regulatory status.  v0.9.9: each open question now carries a trigger_kind aligning it with the flagship §3 typology (7.3 parameterization §3.7; 7.4 open-compositional §3.8; 7.2 resolved-tightening §3.9; 7.1 and 7.5 open-compositional). These are the non-horizon-moving trigger kinds, carried here rather than in the event calendar because none has a commencement to apply by date.

questions.title: Joint NPRM architecture (§404 implementation)
questions.trigger_kind_basis: §3.8-adjacent: the joint NPRM is information/rulemaking whose resolution feeds the routing and market-structure architecture; contingent on CLARITY enactment, no commencement to apply by date.
questions.question: What will the joint Notice of Proposed Rulemaking that CLARITY §404 (as reported) directs the SEC, CFTC, and Treasury to issue within twelve months of enactment actually contain?
questions.affects: The §4 yield-separation architecture and the permitted/prohibited boundary for routing arrangements.
questions.conditional_flag: Depends on enactment of CLARITY substantially as reported and on the joint NPRM's actual content (not yet issued).

questions.title: People's Republic of China boundary stability
questions.trigger_kind_basis: §3.9: the PRC boundary's Feb-2026 written tightening (银发〔2026〕42号) is settled in the tightening direction while a reversing relaxation stays open.
questions.question: The PRC boundary tightened in writing in Feb 2026 (银发〔2026〕42号). Will it hold at that level, tighten further, or relax?
questions.affects: The §3.3 three-pattern typology (especially Pattern A viability).
material_developments: RESOLVED (tightening): 银发〔2026〕42号 (in force 6 Feb 2026) put the prohibition into explicit written law — overseas RMB-pegged-stablecoin issuance banned, extraterritorial issuance by PRC-controlled entities banned, onshore RWA tokenisation restricted (CSRC 公告〔2026〕1号);
STILL-OPEN (relaxation paths): PRC modifies the cross-border data framework to facilitate HKMA supervisor-to-supervisor information-sharing;
the HKMA announces a second cohort including one or more PRC-connected entities;
the PRC publicly authorizes state-owned bank HK affiliates to apply/operate under HK licences.
questions.conditional_flag: The tightening has occurred (42号). None of the three relaxation developments has occurred as of publication; the §3.3 patterns are configured against the boundary as currently observed.

questions.title: Operationalization of the Article 58(3) cap
questions.trigger_kind_basis: §3.7: operationalization of the Article 58(3) means-of-exchange cap — sharpens the operative content of a constraint already in force without moving any class.
questions.question: How will the EBA operationalize the Article 58(3) means-of-exchange cap (adopted methodology guidance and Member State competent-authority practice)?
questions.affects: The §5.14 cross-currency cross-border analysis for USD-referenced stablecoins in the EU.
questions.conditional_flag: The EBA has not finalized the relevant methodology guidance; thresholds are one million daily transactions or EUR 200m daily value (quarterly average, means-of-exchange use).

questions.title: HKMA second-cohort composition
questions.trigger_kind_basis: §3.8: the HKMA second-cohort composition is information, not a change in law; its resolution feeds the PRC boundary-stability question (7.2).
questions.question: What will the composition of the second cohort of HKMA stablecoin licences be (currency coverage; PRC-connected applicants; USD-referenced products)?
questions.affects: US x HK and EU x HK pair feasibility; PRC boundary stability (§7.2).
questions.conditional_flag: The HKMA has not announced the second cohort.

questions.title: Project Agorá (BIS-led) and Project Ensemble (HKMA-led) implications
questions.trigger_kind_basis: §5: the wholesale-settlement experiments are an open compositional/infrastructure question feeding the geopolitical-fracture reading; softer evidence tier, not a class-level trigger.
questions.question: How will the wholesale-settlement experiments (Project Agorá, Project Ensemble, mBridge) interact with private stablecoin architectures at the infrastructure layer?
questions.affects: The Digital-Financial corridor archetype and infrastructure-axis membership across edges.
questions.conditional_flag: Infrastructure implications are forward-looking; the Corridor Atlas develops the infrastructure layer.
